hypothesis: If the integrated thermodynamics–cellular automata–machine learning–market data workflow is applied to Ca2SiO4 synthesis from commercially available reactants at 800–1600°C, then it will identify recipes that both yield Ca2SiO4 as the dominant product and reduce cost per mole relative to heuristic selections because probabilistic reaction-network evolution coupled with thermodynamic screening and supplier pricing accurately captures product formation likelihood and economic efficiency.
